diff_summary: POS cart + discount math: packages/contracts/src/pricing.ts (computeSaleTotals — line% + overall %/KES discounts, per-line VAT split), apps/pos/lib/cart.ts (mutable cart model delegating to computeSaleTotals), apps/pos/app/components/Cart.tsx (cart UI: qty +/-, remove, line/overall discount, live totals, Pay-step stock check), apps/api/src/routes/pos/sales.ts (server recomputes authoritative totals from DB prices). Pinned commit cec2787394 is the epic review-fix (idempotency retry + packer role gating); the cart/discount code itself landed in epic commit 1bd80d8.
ac_coverage: AC1 (lines, qty +/-, remove, line discount %) implemented & tested. AC2 (overall % or KES) implemented & tested. AC3 (live totals + per-line tax per tax_treatment) implemented & tested, BUT the discount/VAT-frame interaction is untested for vat_exclusive/vat_inclusive lines and is wrong for a flat-KES overall discount (see finding 1). AC4 (stock check at Pay, block + clear error) implemented & tested client-side and re-checked server-side. No AC requires discount authorization/caps and none exists (see finding 2).

findings.title: Flat-KES overall discount over-discounts vat_exclusive lines by the VAT fraction (entered KES != actual gross reduction; receipt discount figure matches neither)
findings.detail: computeSaleTotals distributes an overall KES discount across each line's `afterLine` weight and subtracts it in the line's NATIVE price frame, then computes tax on the discounted amount. But `afterLine` is in inconsistent frames across treatments: NET for vat_exclusive, GROSS for vat_inclusive/exempt/zero_rated. For a vat_exclusive line the KES amount is removed from the net base and 16% VAT is then re-added on top, so the customer's actual GROSS reduction != the KES entered. Verified: single vat_exclusive line priceCents 1000 (gross 1160), overall {kind:'kes', valueCents:100} -> grandTotalCents drops from 1160 to 1044, an actual reduction of 116c, while the UI/receipt 'Discount' shows 100c. Mixed cart (one exclusive priceCents 1000 + one inclusive priceCents 1160), overall KES 200 -> gross drops by 215c and discountTotalCents reads 185c — three different numbers (entered 200, shown 185, real 215). Cashier and customer both see one figure while the till takes a different amount; per-treatment behaviour is also non-uniform. Percentage discounts are frame-invariant (10% off gives identical gross for every treatment — verified), so the defect is specific to flat-KES overall discounts touching any vat_exclusive line. Client computeTotals and server computeSaleTotals share the function, so client and server agree on the WRONG amount (no trust-boundary issue, but the recorded sale total is wrong vs. the operator's intent). MONEY-CRITICAL: a stated 'KES X off' does not take KES X off the charge.
findings.location: packages/contracts/src/pricing.ts:96-118 (afterLine weights + discountedLineCents per-line frame); distribution at :139-157
findings.fix: Decide the intended frame for a flat-KES overall discount (almost certainly GROSS = what the customer pays). Then: distribute `overallCents` across each line's GROSS-after-line-discount weight (not the mixed `afterLine`), subtract the share in the gross frame, and derive net/tax from the discounted gross via computeLineTax. That makes entered-KES == actual gross reduction == the displayed discount for every treatment and keeps the subtotal-discount+VAT=total reconciliation. Add tests covering a flat-KES overall discount on vat_exclusive and mixed-treatment carts asserting grandTotal drops by exactly the entered amount (capped). NOTE: the % path is already correct; only the KES path needs the frame fix.

findings.title: No discount cap or authorization — any cashier can apply up to 100% line discount and/or zero out the entire sale with no approval or audit of the discount
findings.detail: The brief flags discount authorization (who can apply a discount / a cap) as money-critical. The schema (posSaleLineSchema.lineDiscountPct max 100, overallDiscountSchema pct max 100 / kes min 0 unbounded) and computeSaleTotals (caps overall KES at the cart total, clamps per-line to >=0) together let a single operator with the 'create payment' permission reduce any sale to KES 0 (100% line discount, 100% overall %, or KES >= total). There is no manager-approval gate, no per-operator discount ceiling, and the discount amount is not surfaced as a distinct audited field — the pos.sale.paid audit payload records only total_cents, not the discount applied or who authorized it. This enables silent shrink/fraud (free goods) with no second-person control. The story spec ACs do not require a cap/approval and the source spec/epics are silent on it, so this is a product gap rather than a violated AC — surfacing per the brief's explicit money-critical focus.
findings.location: packages/contracts/src/index.ts:4572-4583 (no cap beyond 0..100 / >=0); apps/api/src/routes/pos/sales.ts:166-181 (authorize: only create/payment permission, no discount-specific gate); :119-128 (audit payload omits discount/authorizer)
findings.fix: Product decision required: (a) introduce a per-role discount ceiling (e.g. cashier max N% / KES, manager override required above it) enforced server-side, and/or (b) require a manager PIN/approval for discounts over a threshold or for 100%/free sales, and (c) record discountCents + the authorizing principal in the pos.sale.paid audit payload regardless. If the product decision is 'no cap for this phase', record that explicitly and at minimum add the discount amount to the audit trail. Deferring the gate itself, but the missing audit of the discount is a cheap, unambiguous add.
